libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, or system property appears necessary. Meaningful tests can be in-process: exercise XML parsing/serialization, WebDAV method/header/property classes, locators, and servlet-facing contexts with minimal stubs or dynamic proxies rather than starting a servlet container or external WebDAV server.

deterministic_setup.reason: The artifact's public WebDAV servlet/request/response classes use javax.servlet types, but servlet-api is a provided dependency and is not pulled transitively onto the test classpath; the existing 1.5.0 tests already add this dependency to exercise those APIs.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: If tests avoid servlet-facing classes, coverage will miss a central part of the library's normal server-side usage.
The issue is a native-image runtime failure, so Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative even though no external service setup is indicated.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jackrabbit-webdav exposes servlet-based request/response/context APIs and declares javax.servlet:servlet-api only as a provided dependency, so generated tests need the servlet API added explicitly to reach meaningful coverage.
